metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4754
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: biochemical fractions of ER-enriched and synaptic membranes show FRRS1L is present on both but enriched in the ER.

Figure 2: Stepwise co-expression in cultured cells showed FRRS1L is able to form protein complexes together with GRIA1, GRIA2 and and various ER-expressed AMPAR auxilliary subunits.

A minority (~30%) of surface-expressed FRRS1L in mouse dissociated hippocampal neuron cultures co-localizes with PSD95/DLG4 (W. Han et al. 2017, PMID:29276473), so it stands to reason that some (but not all) ER localized FRSS1L is localized at or very near postsynapses. Based on the known transmembrane topology of this protein, "integral component of ... membrane" term was selected.. Experimental description: M&M: "Adult mice (of both sex) were used for biochemical, electrophysiological, and behavioral experiments"
